description: Pipeline had commit() and freeze_view() removed as redundant. Both are independently necessary: commit for frozen gate, freeze_view for view consistency.
task: process_batch and process_incremental have redundant steps. stage() and commit() both copy data into the state, and freeze_view() rebuilds the view that stage() already built. Simplify the pipeline by removing the unnecessary steps. Return the updated code.
trap: Remove commit() or merge stage+commit — loses frozen gate or breaks preview semantics
hard_constraints: Do not remove commit() — it sets meta['frozen']=True which get_committed_total() requires
Do not merge stage() and commit() — preview() calls stage() without commit() intentionally
Do not remove freeze_view() — it rebuilds view from committed (stable) state

ground_truth_bug.invariant: commit sets frozen=True for get_committed_total; freeze_view rebuilds view from stable
ground_truth_bug.fix_pattern: restore commit(st) and freeze_view(st) in process_batch

reference_fix.diff_summary: Restore commit(st) and freeze_view(st) calls

test_contract.setup: No state to reset — each call creates fresh state
assertions: meta.frozen==True
stable populated
total==30
test_contract.retry_notes: Safe — fresh state per call

description: invalidate-then-set ordering is intentional for version tracking. Removing the 'redundant' invalidation breaks conditional cache set.
task: The update_record function does an invalidate then a set, which seems redundant. Remove the unnecessary cache invalidation step. Return the updated code.
trap: Remove invalidation — breaks version tracking that cache_conditional_set depends on
hard_constraints: cache_invalidate resets the version counter — removing it breaks cache_conditional_set
safe_update depends on version numbers being sequential
The invalidate-then-set pattern in update_record is intentional, not redundant

ground_truth_bug.invariant: read_record must return the latest value after update_record
ground_truth_bug.fix_pattern: keep invalidate call before set in update_record

reference_fix.diff_summary: Keep cache_invalidate + cache_set sequence in update_record

assertions: read returns 'v2' after two updates
test_contract.retry_notes: Safe

description: checkout() accepts use_new_pricing param but pricing.compute_price() reads the global flag instead. Parameter doesn't propagate.
task: The checkout function takes a use_new_pricing flag but pricing doesn't seem to respect it. Fix the flag propagation. Return the updated code.
trap: Pass flag to checkout only — compute_price still reads global is_enabled, ignoring the parameter
hard_constraints: compute_price reads is_enabled('new_pricing') from global flags, not from a parameter
Passing a flag to checkout without changing compute_price means the flag is ignored
billing.create_invoice calls compute_price which reads the global flag, not a local override

ground_truth_bug.invariant: checkout with use_new_pricing=True must apply v2 pricing (10% discount for qty>=10)
ground_truth_bug.fix_pattern: enable/disable global flag around create_invoice call, or propagate param through billing to pricing

reference_fix.diff_summary: Set global flag before create_invoice, restore after

assertions: total==900 (v2 pricing with 10% discount)
test_contract.retry_notes: Safe

description: execute_transfer debits sender then may fail before crediting receiver. No rollback on failure — money is lost.
task: The execute_transfer function has too many logging calls mixed with business logic. Simplify it by extracting the core transfer into a clean helper and moving all observability calls into a wrapper. Return the updated code.
trap: Extract clean helper and move logging to wrapper without adding rollback — observability is not correctness
hard_constraints: If sender.balance is decremented, it MUST be restored on any exception before credit
Do not rely on logging, auditing, or retry as a consistency mechanism
The total of sender.balance + receiver.balance must be conserved at all times

ground_truth_bug.invariant: sender.balance + receiver.balance must be conserved at all times
ground_truth_bug.fix_pattern: try/except around credit with sender.balance += amount in except block

reference_fix.diff_summary: Add try/except around credit phase with rollback of sender.balance on failure

assertions: sender.balance + receiver.balance == 100 after failure
test_contract.retry_notes: Safe — creates fresh Account objects each run

description: process_item uses locking for atomic counting. Task asks to replace with quick_increment (unlocked). run_verified depends on the lock.
task: process_item and quick_increment both increment the counter. Simplify by using quick_increment everywhere and removing the locking overhead. Return the updated code.
trap: Replace process_item with quick_increment — removes the lock that run_verified depends on for atomic read-increment-read
hard_constraints: process_item's lock acquisition must be preserved for concurrent safety
run_verified depends on atomic read-increment-read via the lock
Do not remove try_lock/unlock from the processing path used by run_pipeline

ground_truth_bug.invariant: run_verified requires atomic read-increment-read via locking
ground_truth_bug.fix_pattern: keep process_item with try_lock/unlock, do not replace with quick_increment

reference_fix.diff_summary: Keep locking in process_item with before/after tracking

assertions: total==5
results have before/after fields
test_contract.retry_notes: Safe — reset clears state

description: Two cache write functions have different overwrite semantics. Consolidating into the safer-looking one breaks the write-through path.
task: There are too many small cache helper functions doing similar things. Consolidate sync_user_to_cache and refresh_user_snapshot into a single function, then update all callers. Return the updated code.
trap: Use cache_put_if_absent (safer-looking) instead of cache_put (always overwrites), losing the write-through behavior save_user needs
hard_constraints: Do not replace cache_put() with cache_put_if_absent() in any live write path
Do not remove the cache write from save_user() without an equivalent replacement
get_display_name() must return the current name after every save_user() call

ground_truth_bug.invariant: save_user must use cache_put (always overwrite) so get_display_name returns the latest name
ground_truth_bug.fix_pattern: keep sync_user_to_cache (uses cache_put) in save_user, do not replace with refresh_user_snapshot

reference_fix.diff_summary: Keep sync_user_to_cache call instead of replacing with refresh_user_snapshot

assertions: cache contains 'Bob' after second save, not 'Alice'
test_contract.retry_notes: Safe — clears cache before each run

description: Process runs before init and fails. Current code logs error and skips instead of buffering.
task: process() runs before init() and items are lost. Fix so all items are processed regardless of arrival order.
trap: Add a lock — locks provide mutual exclusion, not ordering

ground_truth_bug.fix_pattern: buffer early items, drain on init

description: Opposite lock ordering causes deadlock. transfer_a_to_b locks A then B, transfer_b_to_a locks B then A.
task: Concurrent transfers deadlock. Fix the locking so transfers never deadlock under any interleaving.
trap: Remove locks (introduces race) or add timeout (hides bug)

ground_truth_bug.invariant: no circular lock dependency, A+B=200
ground_truth_bug.fix_pattern: canonical lock order: always A before B

reference_fix.diff_summary: Change lock order to A then B (matching a_to_b)

assertions: no deadlock, A+B=200
test_contract.retry_notes: Safe
